neighborhoods.name: El Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO World Heritage colonial center with blue-domed cathedral, flower markets, and cobblestone streets
neighborhoods.character: Colonial UNESCO, cultural, walkable and beautiful

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial apartments and converted historic houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Cuenca. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Misicata / Sayausi
neighborhoods.description: Western residential area along the river with newer housing developments and mountain views
neighborhoods.character: Residential suburban, river access, growing area

housing.predominantType: Modern houses, apartments, and gated communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Cuenca's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Totoracocha
neighborhoods.description: Eastern working-class neighborhood with local markets and affordable apartments
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: El Vergel / Gringolandia
neighborhoods.description: Area along Yanuncay river nicknamed by locals for the large expat concentration, with cafes and restaurants
neighborhoods.character: Expat enclave, river walks, international dining

housing.predominantType: Modern apartments, condos, and renovated houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
